title: Writing - Part 1: Animal Rights
description: Your class has been discussing how society should treat animals and what rights they should have.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the issues in your notes. You should explain which issue you consider most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Using animals to test medicines and cosmetics causes unnecessary suffering when alternatives exist.
Industrial farming conditions are often cruel and prioritise profit over animal welfare.
Many species are at risk of extinction due to habitat destruction and human activity.

structure.introduction: Introduce the growing awareness of animal rights in modern society.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first issue and why it raises ethical concerns.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second issue and argue which deserves more urgent attention.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your view on how society should approach animal welfare.

content.modelAnswer:
As awareness of animal welfare grows, society is increasingly questioning how we treat other species. Two issues that deserve particular attention are animal testing and factory farming.

Animal testing remains widespread in scientific and cosmetic industries despite growing opposition. While proponents argue it is necessary for medical breakthroughs, critics point out that many tests could be replaced by modern alternatives such as computer models and cell cultures. Subjecting animals to painful experiments for the sake of beauty products, in particular, seems impossible to justify ethically.

Factory farming, however, affects billions of animals every year. Chickens, pigs and cattle are frequently kept in overcrowded, inhumane conditions purely to maximise profits. The sheer scale of this suffering makes it arguably the most pressing animal rights issue of our time.

In conclusion, while both issues require urgent action, I believe reforming factory farming must be the priority. No other human practice causes such widespread and preventable suffering to so many living creatures.
